channel.title: Programming on blogu geegu.
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on blogu geegu.

item.title: Setting up Elixir in github-actions for windows
item.description: When our Elixir code is directly interacting with host operating system, it is nice to have automated tests integrated for all targeted operating systems. Github actions makes this task easier than before but unfortunately elixir-setup does not support operating systems other than ubuntu yet. I tried to find a solution for this for one of my elixir library Using Chocolatey package manager We can use Chocolatey Action install elixir. Chocolatey also installs Erlang as elixir dependency for us.

item.title: Elixir Gotcha #1
item.description: Can you guess the output of this code snippet? def baz(_a) do puts "baz/1" end def baz(_a, _b) do puts "baz/2" end baz(name: "foo", age: 30) Answer is baz/1. If you have worked with elixir for some time you will know about keyword lists. Hex docs define it as, Keyword is a list of two-element tuples where the first element of the tuple is an atom and the second element can be any value.
